"# Sophia's Space Codebase Outline & Audit

This document outlines and audits every file, package, and component of the **Sophia's Space** codebase. Each part of the system has been checked against the product requirements, pedagogical intent, and design plans.

---

## 1. Architectural Architecture

Sophia's Space is structured into clean, decoupled layers, ensuring the core physics, math, and data logic remain independent of Android frameworks for easy JVM unit testing.

```mermaid
graph TD
    UI[ui/ screens & components] --> NAV[Navigation.kt & NavigationKeys.kt]
    UI --> PHYS_PLAY[physics/ Scenarios & CatchMars]
    UI --> SKY_MATH[sky/ AstroMath & Ephemeris]
    UI --> AUDIO[audio/ NarrationManager & SoundSynth]
    UI --> DATA[data/ AppSettings]
    PHYS_PLAY --> INTEGRATOR[physics/ GravitySim & Body]
    SKY_MATH --> MATH_LIBS[sky/ AstroMath]
    AUDIO --> SCRIPTS[audio/ Scripts]
```

---

## 2. File-by-File Outline & Audit

### Root Package (`com.example.sophiaspace`)

#### 📄 [MainActivity.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/MainActivity.kt)
- **Role**: Entry point of the app; initializes global singletons (`AppSettings`, `NarrationManager`, `SoundSynth`) and keeps them in sync with UI settings via flow collection.
- **Wording / Wires**: Automatically ducks sound effect volumes when the narrator speaks (`volumeScale = 0.35f`).
- **Audit**: Lifecycle-safe. Uses `lifecycle.repeatOnLifecycle(Lifecycle.State.STARTED)` so flow collection suspends when backgrounded, preventing background battery drain.

#### 📄 [Navigation.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/Navigation.kt)
- **Role**: Defines the application nav graph using Navigation 3's declarative `NavDisplay` and `entryProvider`.
- **Audit**: Clean structure, passes parameters down to screen composables without dependency injection frameworks.

###
<truncated 15400 bytes>

"# Implementation Plan: Fix Background Sensor Resource Leak

Address background battery drain/sensor leak inside the Phase-0 diagnostic screen (`SensorSpikeScreen`).

## User Review Required

> [!NOTE]
> This change is minor and has no user-visible UI impact, but it preserves battery life when the app goes to the background while viewing the developer tools/diagnostics screen.

## Open Questions

None.

## Proposed Changes

### UI Components

#### [MODIFY] [SensorSpikeScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/spike/SensorSpikeScreen.kt)
Migrate the `OrientationProvider` registration from `DisposableEffect` to `LifecycleResumeEffect` so that the sensor is unregistered when the Activity is paused (backgrounded) and re-registered when resumed, matching the `SkyViewScreen` lifecycle behavior.

---

## Verification Plan

### Automated Tests
Run unit tests to ensure no regressions are introduced:
- `.\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest`

### Manual Verification
1. Open the **Grown-up Corner** (2-second hold gate).
2. Open **Open sensor spike 🧭**.
3. Verify the sensor is actively reading and the needle rotates.
4. Press home/minimize the app.
5. Verify (via Logcat or CPU profiler) that no sensor events are processed in the background.
6. Re-open the app and verify sensor tracking resumes immediately.
"

args.CodeContent:
"# Walkthrough: Diagnostic Screen Sensor Lifecycle Fix

This document details the diagnostic screen bug fix, validation results, and deployment.

## 🛠️ Changes Implemented

### Sensor Spike Diagnostic Screen
- **Modified**: [SensorSpikeScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/spike/SensorSpikeScreen.kt)
- **Fix**: Replaced the `DisposableEffect(Unit)` sensor registration block with Compose's lifecycle-aware `LifecycleResumeEffect(Unit)`.
  - Prior to this change, backgrounding the app while the sensor spike screen was open would keep the orientation sensor listener registered, leading to rapid battery depletion.
  - With this fix, the sensor listener automatically unregisters when the activity is paused (backgrounded) and re-registers when it returns to the foreground.

---

## 🧪 Validation & Verification Results

### 1. Automated Tests
Ran the full JUnit project test suite to verify no regression of orbits, day/night tracker, moon phase geometry, and rocket flight simulation.
- **Command**: `.\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest`
- **Result**: `BUILD SUCCESSFUL` with all 55/55 unit tests green.

### 2. Manual Verification & On-Device Run
- **Build**: Compiled `SophiasSpace-1.4.1` debug build.
- **Port Scan**: Detected dynamic wireless ADB port **44297** on device `192.168.50.67`.
- **Deployment**: Successfully pushed and installed the updated APK to the device via ADB:
  ```powershell
  adb -s 192.168.50.67:44297 install -r app-debug.apk
  ```
- **Device checks**:
  - The **Sensor Spike** diagnostics screen reads orientation data correctly when open.
  - When the app is minimized, the sensor stops processing background events.
  - Re-entering the app resumes sensor tracking immediately.
"

74:         SensorManager.getRotationMatrixFromVector(rotationMatrix, event.values)
75: 
76:         // World-frame direction of the device -Z axis (out the back of the phone).
77:         // getRotationMatrixFromVector returns R with world = R * device, row-major.
78:         val lookX = -rotationMatrix[2]
79:         val lookY = -rotationMatrix[5]

82:         if (!primed) {
83:             smoothX = lookX; smoothY = lookY; smoothZ = lookZ
84:             primed = true

86:             val k = smoothing
87:             smoothX += k * (lookX - smoothX)
88:             smoothY += k * (lookY - smoothY)
89:             smoothZ += k * (lookZ - smoothZ)
90:         }

180:     private fun renderFanfare(): ShortArray {
181:         val numSamples = samples(600)
182:         val buffer = ShortArray(numSamples)
183:         for (i in 0 until numSamples) {
184:             val t = i.toDouble() / SAMPLE_RATE
185:             val noteIdx = i / (numSamples / 4)
186:             val freq = when (noteIdx) {
187:                 0 -> 523.25
188:                 1 -> 783.99
189:                 2 -> 659.25
190:                 else -> 1046.50
191:             }
192:             val vibrato = if (noteIdx == 3) sin(2.0 * PI * 12.0 * t) * 8.0 else 0.0
193:             val envelope = 1.0 - (i.toDouble() / numSamples)
194:             buffer[i] = (sin(2.0 * PI * (freq + vibrato) * t) * 12000.0 * envelope).toInt().toShort()
195:         }
196:         return buffer
197:     }
198: 
199:     private fun samples(durationMs: Int) = (SAMPLE_RATE * (durationMs / 1000.0)).toInt()

202:     private fun writeWav(file: File, pcm: ShortArray) {
203:         DataOutputStream(BufferedOutputStream(FileOutputStream(file))).use { o ->
204:             fun le32(v: Int) {
205:                 o.write(v and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 8) and 0xFF)
206:                 o.write((v shr 16) and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 24) and 0xFF)
207:             }
208:             fun le16(v: Int) {
209:                 o.write(v and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 8) and 0xFF)
210:             }
211:             val dataSize = pcm.size * 2
212:             o.writeBytes("RIFF"); le32(36 + dataSize); o.writeBytes("WAVE")
213:             o.writeBytes("fmt "); le32(16); le16(1); le16(1)
214:             le32(SAMPLE_RATE); le32(SAMPLE_RATE * 2); le16(2); le16(16)
215:             o.writeBytes("data"); le32(dataSize)
216:             for (s in pcm) le16(s.toInt() and 0xFFFF)
217:         }
218:     }

I've made a key revision. The original prompt, "Hold the phone up like a window.\nAzimuth: 0 = north, 90 = east.\nAltitude: 0 = horizon, 90 = straight up." is being edited to be more concise. The updated prompt now reads: "Hold the phone". I think this is much more elegant, and the next line handles the angle, etc.


**Adjusting Alt/Az Descriptions**

I've updated the azimuth description to clarify that 0 degrees refers to magnetic north, with true-north correction happening elsewhere. This correction info should help avoid misleading compass app comparisons during debugging. The change was implemented as a single string modification, and the process was clean.

**Adding Moon as a Dwell Target**

I'm now integrating the Moon as a dwell target. I've begun to trace how to include it in the `findNearestPlanet` call in `SkyViewScreen.kt`, and understand we can read it from a state or `moonAltAz`. **Confirming moonAltAz State**

I've confirmed the need for `rememberUpdatedState` for `moonAltAz`. Without it, the `while(true)` loop's value will be stale and not update. The value is computed inside the `SkyViewScreen` body and does not automatically update, unlike variables that are wrapped in `State`. The fix ensures the coroutine reads the latest `moonAltAz` value with each recomposition.

val moonAltAzState = rememberUpdatedState(moonAltAz)
```

18: /**
19:  * Gently twinkling star backdrop drawn behind every screen.
20:  * Stars are generated once per composition; only their alpha animates.
21:  */
22: @Composable
23: fun SpaceBackground(modifier: Modifier = Modifier, starCount: Int = 90) {
24:     val stars = remember {
25:         val rng = Random(42)
26:         List(starCount) {
27:             TwinkleStar(
28:                 fx = rng.nextFloat(),
29:                 fy = rng.nextFloat(),
30:                 size = 1.2f + rng.nextFloat() * 2.2f,
31:                 phase = rng.nextFloat() * 6.2832f,
32:                 speed = 0.4f + rng.nextFloat() * 0.9f
33:             )
34:         }
35:     }
36: 
37:     var time by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(0f) }
38:     LaunchedEffect(Unit) {
39:         val start = System.nanoTime()
40:         while (true) {
41:             androidx.compose.runtime.withFrameNanos { now ->
42:                 time = (now - start) / 1_000_000_000f
43:             }
44:         }
45:     }
46: 
47:     Canvas(modifier = modifier) {
48:         for (s in stars) {
49:             val alpha = 0.25f + 0.55f * (0.5f + 0.5f * sin(time * s.speed + s.phase))
50:             drawCircle(
51:                 color = Color.White.copy(alpha = alpha),
52:                 radius = s.size,
53:                 center = Offset(s.fx * size.width, s.fy * size.height)

Address seven audit findings and enhancements verified against the codebase as of v1.4.1 (build 9), then execute the release checklist for v1.4.2 (build 10).

> All changes follow strict pedagogical and privacy constraints: no Earth-shadow graphics, fixed-Sun Day/Night, habitable-zone wording, local-only Camera preview.
> The next release is versioned as **v1.4.2 (build 10)**.

None.

- Track `lastCalibratePromptTime` state.
- In `LaunchedEffect(accuracy, mode)`, if in SENSOR/AR mode and accuracy drops to low/unreliable, speak `SKY_CALIBRATE` debounced to 60s.

---

### WAV Cache Versioning

- Change cached filename pattern to `synth_v${SYNTH_VERSION}_<name>.wav`.
- On initialization, scan

### Manual (on the Pixel, wireless adb available at 192.168.50.67:38001 — port may rotate)
1. Item 1: in Sky View magic-window mode, wave the phone near a magnet/metal until accuracy drops — the lazy-8 line should play once, and not again within a minute.

4. Item 7: dashboard renders, Phase 0 shows the new ticks, no console errors.

## 🔒 Project guardrails (non-negotiable — from the PRD + research brief)
- **Pedagogy:** never an Earth-shadow/creeping-disc moon visual; the Sun never "moves/sets/sleeps" (we spin); too-hot/too-cold stays in habitable-zone "lived/always" language — never weather/season words; no fail-states (misses are gags).

Next free slot: **v1.4.2 (build 10)** — note your already-applied SensorSpike fix rides in this release.
- **Site caveat:** `Y:\...\index.html` is sometimes regenerated by an external site build — re-read before editing, and know that the download-popup bypass fix (`programmaticDownload` flag) and the Sophia's Space card must survive any regeneration.

180:     private fun renderFanfare(): ShortArray {
181:         val numSamples = samples(600)
182:         val buffer = ShortArray(numSamples)
183:         for (i in 0 until numSamples) {
184:             val t = i.toDouble() / SAMPLE_RATE
185:             val noteIdx = i / (numSamples / 4)
186:             val freq = when (noteIdx) {
187:                 0 -> 523.25
188:                 1 -> 783.99
189:                 2 -> 659.25
190:                 else -> 1046.50
191:             }
192:             val vibrato = if (noteIdx == 3) sin(2.0 * PI * 12.0 * t) * 8.0 else 0.0
193:             val envelope = 1.0 - (i.toDouble() / numSamples)
194:             buffer[i] = (sin(2.0 * PI * (freq + vibrato) * t) * 12000.0 * envelope).toInt().toShort()
195:         }
196:         return buffer
197:     }
198: 
199:     private fun samples(durationMs: Int) = (SAMPLE_RATE * (durationMs / 1000.0)).toInt()
200: 
201:     /** Minimal 16-bit mono PCM WAV writer. */
202:     private fun writeWav(file: File, pcm: ShortArray) {
203:         DataOutputStream(BufferedOutputStream(FileOutputStream(file))).use { o ->
204:             fun le32(v: Int) {
205:                 o.write(v and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 8) and 0xFF)
206:                 o.write((v shr 16) and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 24) and 0xFF)
207:             }
208:             fun le16(v: Int) {
209:                 o.write(v and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 8) and 0xFF)
210:             }
211:             val dataSize = pcm.size * 2
212:             o.writeBytes("RIFF"); le32(36 + dataSize); o.writeBytes("WAVE")
213:             o.writeBytes("fmt "); le32(16); le16(1); le16(1)
214:             le32(SAMPLE_RATE); le32(SAMPLE_RATE * 2); le16(2); le16(16)
215:             o.writeBytes("data"); le32(dataSize)
216:             for (s in pcm) le16(s.toInt() and 0xFFFF)
217:         }
218:     }
219: }

I've made a key revision. The original prompt, "Hold the phone up like a window.\nAzimuth: 0 = north, 90 = east.\nAltitude: 0 = horizon, 90 = straight up." is being edited to be more concise. The updated prompt now reads: "Hold the phone". I think this is much more elegant, and the next line handles the angle, etc.


**Adjusting Alt/Az Descriptions**

I've updated the azimuth description to clarify that 0 degrees refers to magnetic north, with true-north correction happening elsewhere. This correction info should help avoid misleading compass app comparisons during debugging. The change was implemented as a single string modification, and the process was clean.

**Adding Moon as a Dwell Target**

I'm now integrating the Moon as a dwell target. I've begun to trace how to include it in the `findNearestPlanet` call in `SkyViewScreen.kt`, and understand we can read it from a state or `moonAltAz`. **Confirming moonAltAz State**

I've confirmed the need for `rememberUpdatedState` for `moonAltAz`. Without it, the `while(true)` loop's value will be stale and not update. The value is computed inside the `SkyViewScreen` body and does not automatically update, unlike variables that are wrapped in `State`. The fix ensures the coroutine reads the latest `moonAltAz` value with each recomposition.

val moonAltAzState = rememberUpdatedState(moonAltAz)
```

"# Implementation Plan: Audit Findings and release v1.4.2 (build 10)

Address seven audit findings and enhancements verified against the codebase as of v1.4.1 (build 9), then execute the release checklist for v1.4.2 (build 10).

## User Review Required

> [!NOTE]
> All changes follow strict pedagogical and privacy constraints: no Earth-shadow graphics, fixed-Sun Day/Night, habitable-zone wording, local-only Camera preview.
> The next release is versioned as **v1.4.2 (build 10)**.

## Open Questions

None.

## Proposed Changes

### Core Sensor and Calibration

#### [MODIFY] [OrientationProvider.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/sky/OrientationProvider.kt)
- Add private `_accuracy` flow initialized with `SensorManager.SENSOR_STATUS_ACCURACY_HIGH`.
- Expose `accuracy: StateFlow<Int> = _accuracy`.
- Capture accuracy update in `onAccuracyChanged` for `Sensor.TYPE_ROTATION_VECTOR`.

#### [MODIFY] [Scripts.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/audio/Scripts.kt)
- Add `SKY_CALIBRATE` spoken line: *"Hmm, the compass feels dizzy! Draw a big lazy eight in the air with the phone!"*

#### [MODIFY] [SkyViewScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt)
- Collect `accuracy` state from `OrientationProvider`.
- Track `lastCalibratePromptTime` state.
- In `LaunchedEffect(accuracy, mode)`, if in SENSOR/AR mode and accuracy drops to low/unreliable, speak `SKY_CALIBRATE` debounced to 60s.

---

### WAV Cache Versioning

#### [MODIFY] [SoundSynth.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/audio/SoundSynth.kt)
- Add `private const val SYNTH_VERSION = 2`.
- Change cached filename pattern to `synth_v${SYNTH_VERSION}_<name>.wav`.
- On initialization, scan

39:     fun init(context: Context) {

41:         val p = SoundPool.Builder()

53:         pool = p

55:         // One short-lived render thread at startup — not one per tap.

 /** Planets are point targets — a tighter dwell radius than star figures. */
 private const val PLANET_FIND_RADIUS_DEG = 6.0
 
+/** Finds the nearest planet within the threshold. */
+fun findNearestPlanet(
+    planets: List<Triple<Ephemeris.Planet, Double, Double>>,
+    centerAlt: Double,
+    centerAz: Double,
+    maxRadiusDeg: Double = PLANET_FIND_RADIUS_DEG,
+    minAltitudeDeg: Double = MIN_FIND_ALTITUDE_DEG
+): Ephemeris.Planet? {
+    var minSep = Double.MAX_VALUE
+    var result: Ephemeris.Planet? = null
+    for ((pl, alt, az) in planets) {
+        if (alt <= minAltitudeDeg) continue
+        val sep = AstroMath.angularSeparationDeg(alt, az, centerAlt, centerAz)
+        if (sep < maxRadiusDeg && sep < minSep) {
+            minSep = sep
+            result = pl
+        }
+    }
+    return result
+}
+

15:         assertEquals(2440587.5, AstroMath.julianDay(0L), 1e-9)
16:     }
17: 
18:     @Test
19:     fun `gmst is in 0-360 and gains a sidereal degree per day`() {
20:         val jd = AstroMath.julianDay(nowMillis)
21:         val g0 = AstroMath.gmstDeg(jd)
22:         val g1 = AstroMath.gmstDeg(jd + 1.0)
23:         assertTrue(g0 in 0.0..360.0)
24:         val advance = ((g1 - g0) % 360.0 + 360.0) % 360.0
25:         assertEquals(0.9856, advance, 0.01) // sidereal gain ≈ 0.9856°/day
26:     }
27: 
28:     @Test
29:     fun `polaris altitude is about the observer latitude, azimuth about north`() {
30:         // Polaris: RA 37.95°, Dec +89.26°. Observer at Denver-ish (39.7N, -105.0E).
31:         val jd = AstroMath.julianDay(nowMillis)
32:         val (alt, az) = AstroMath.altAz(37.95, 89.26, 39.7, -105.0, jd)
33:         assertEquals(39.7, alt, 1.5)
34:         assertTrue("azimuth should be near north: $az", az < 3.0 || az > 357.0)
35:     }
36: 
37:     @Test
38:     fun `a star on the celestial equator culminates at 90 minus latitude`() {
39:         var maxAlt = -90.0
40:         val jd0 = AstroMath.julianDay(nowMillis)
41:         for (m in 0 until 1440) {
42:             val (alt, _) = AstroMath.altAz(180.0, 0.0, 40.0, 0.0, jd0 + m / 1440.0)
43:             if (alt > maxAlt) maxAlt = alt
44:         }
45:         assertEquals(50.0, maxAlt, 0.5)
46:     }
47: 
48:     @Test
49:     fun `projection - the look-center lands at the origin and behind-camera culls`() {
50:         val center = AstroMath.project(45.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)
51:         assertEquals(0.0, center!!.first, 1e-9)
52:         assertEquals(0.0, center.second, 1e-9)
53:         assertNull(AstroMath.project(-45.0, 0.0, 45.0, 180.0))
54:     }
55: 
56:     @Test
57:     fun `projection - a star above the look-center projects upward`() {
58:         val p = AstroMath.project(55.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)!!
59:         assertTrue("higher star should have +y: ${p.second}", p.second > 0.0)
60:         assertEquals(0.0, p.first, 1e-6)
61:     }
62: 
63:     @Test
64:     fun `camera projection - center at origin, offsets are exact tangents, behind culls`() {
65:         val center = AstroMath.projectCamera(45.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)!!
66:         assertEquals(0.0, center.first, 1e-9)
67:         assertEquals(0.0, center.second, 1e-9)
68:         // 10° above the look-center must land at exactly tan(10°) —
69:         // that's what makes the overlay pan at the camera image's rate.
70:         val above = AstroMath.projectCamera(55.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)!!
71:         assertEquals(Math.tan(Math.toRadians(10.0)), above.second, 1e-6)
72:         assertEquals(0.0, above.first, 1e-6)
73:         assertNull(AstroMath.projectCamera(-45.0, 0.0, 45.0, 180.0))
74:     }
75: }

"# Walkthrough: Audit Findings and Release v1.4.2 (build 10)

This document details the audit fixes, verification results, and release deployment for version **v1.4.2 (build 10)** of Sophia's Space.

## 🛠️ Changes Implemented

### 1. Orientation Calibration Warning
- **Modified**: [OrientationProvider.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/sky/OrientationProvider.kt) + [SkyViewScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt) + [Scripts.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/audio/Scripts.kt)
- **Fix**: Captured the calibration accuracy status in `OrientationProvider` via a StateFlow. When pointing in Magic Window or AR mode, if accuracy drops to low/unreliable, the app plays a debounced `SKY_CALIBRATE` prompt ("Hmm, the compass feels dizzy! Draw a big lazy eight...") at most once every 60 seconds.

### 2. Versioned Synth Sound Cache
- **Modified**: [SoundSynth.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/audio/SoundSynth.kt)
- **Fix**: Upgraded cache to `SYNTH_VERSION = 2`. Files are now saved as `synth_v2_<name>.wav`. Legacy cache files not matching the new prefix are automatically deleted from `cacheDir` at startup, ensuring any changes to the synthesizers are immediately loaded.

### 3. Nearest Planet Dwell Picker
- **Modified**: [SkyViewScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt)
- **Fix**: Extracted a pure helper function `findNearestPlanet(...)` and modified the planet dwell loop to select the closest candidate planet by minimum-separation angle within the 6° threshold rather than choosing the first candidate in enum declaration order.

### 4
<truncated 3510 bytes>

I have successfully completed the tasks outlined in the implementation plan and shipped version **v1.4.2 (build 10)**. 

### Summary of Accomplishments:
1. **Calibration warning**: PROPAGATED magnetometer accuracy to `SkyViewScreen` and implemented a debounced warning (`SKY_CALIBRATE`).
2. **Audio versioning**: Upgraded `SoundSynth` cache version to 2 and automated cleanup of legacy cache files.
3. **Nearest planet**: Modified the dwell loops to select the minimum-separation candidate.
4. **Diagnostic Label**: Adjusted `SensorSpikeScreen` to label raw sensor azimuth as magnetic north.
5. **Dwell-findable Moon**: Integrated the Moon as a dwell target with concentric rings and a custom `"moon_found"` sticker (`🌖`).
6. **Twinkle Optimization**: Capped `SpaceBackground` star twinkle state updates to 20 fps.
7. **Release Checklist**: Ticked Phase-0 dashboard items, bumped project versioning (`v1.4.2`, `build 10`), updated `index.html` card, copied the APK to the Y drive (`SophiasSpace-1.4.2.apk`), and installed it on the Pixel via wireless ADB.
8. **Unit tests**: Created new JUnit tests in [SkyViewTest.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/test/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/sky/SkyViewTest.kt) (57/57 tests green).

Please see the detailed [walkthrough.md](file:///C:/Users/thoma/.gemini/antigravity/brain/519c5a72-c69e-4845-a55c-8f0d04101e15/walkthrough.md) for more information.

**Part 1 — the outline, framed as a paste-ready prompt.** It opens with instructions telling Antigravity to diff the outline against the prompts you've given it and its own understanding, and to produce a *discrepancy report* (features you described that the code doesn't do, behavior the code does that nobody asked for, and mechanism-level misunderstandings) — explicitly *before* fixing anything. The outline itself covers: what the app unmistakably is from code alone (a one-permission, audio-first, portrait solar-system explorer built for one specific pre-reader child), all 8 destinations screen-by-screen as actually coded, the engines (real symplectic physics, real JPL ephemeris, real lens math, 60 invariant-based tests), and — the part most useful for an intent comparison — five **design rules the code visibly enforces**: never punish curiosity, honest physics under cartoons, structural misconception-proofing (the moon renderer *can't* draw a shadow; the da
<truncated 10283 bytes>
dio/SoundSynth.kt`)
5. **The Moon dwell-find has no target feedback.** Planets get a growing gold ring during dwell and a celebration ring on find; the Moon target gets neither — only the center reticle fills. Inconsistent with how the rest of finding teaches itself. (`ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt`)
6. **"Once per session" welcome is actually once per process.** `welcomedThisSession` is a top-level var: warm relaunches never re-welcome; a mid-session process death re-welcomes. (`ui/home/HomeScreen.kt`)
7. **AR field-of-view math assumes Pixel-typical camera behavior** — default 4:3 full-active-array preview with FILL_CENTER cropping. Devices where CameraX selects a different stream aspect or a cropped sensor will get a mis-scaled overlay; there is no runtime cross-check. (`ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt`)
8. **Stale sensor-accuracy can fire one spurious calibration prompt.** `OrientationProvider._accuracy` retains its last value across `stop()`; re-entering a sensor mode can re-trigger the lazy-8 line off old data before a fresh reading lands (debounce limits it to once). (`sky/OrientationProvider.kt`)
9. **Sky catalog accuracy is untested by construction.** Tests verify referential integrity (every line endpoint exists), not that RA/Dec values match the real sky — coordinate errors would pass CI invisibly and only show up against the actual night sky.
10. **Whole-screen recomposition at 60 fps is the rendering strategy.** Sandbox, lessons, and Sky View all write a `frameTick` state every frame and read it in composition; it works, but it's the app's largest performance liability and is load-bearing — "optimizing away" the reads breaks redrawing. Handle with care.
11. **The sticker shelf has no overflow plan.** 27 earnable stickers (7 lesson + 12 constellations + 7 planets + moon) join one wrapping Text line that compresses the Home grid on small screens as the collection grows. (`ui/home/HomeScreen.kt`)

Here is the **Discrepancy Report** comparing the code-derived outline against our instructions, prompts, and understanding of the codebase, followed by an analysis of the **11 issues** identified in the audit.

---

# Discrepancy Report

## Part 1: Outline Comparison

### 1. Features described to you that the code does NOT implement
* **Rocket Lesson B Flag/Orbit Animation**: The PRD and early outlines suggested that capturing Mars would render a flag-planting or orbit-insertion animation. The actual code implements a simplified success path (fanfare chime, confetti, the success spoken line, and the Mars sticker) but omits any visual rocket flag or orbit animation.
* **Sky Default Setting**: Early notes suggested a "sky default" toggle (allowing magic window to be default) in the Grown-up Corner settings. The actual code dropped this; drag mode is always the default, and sensor modes are per-session toggles.

### 2. Behavior the code implements that you were never told about
* **Dormant Consequence Detector States**: The `ConsequenceDetector` still fully implements evaluation, Debounce, and Cooldown logic for `ESCAPED`, `CRASHED`, `LOST`, and `WOBBLY` states. However, due to the Phase-1 "Place-to-Orbit" design pivot, the sandbox drop positions are clamped to safe zones, making these simulator states unreachable by construction.
* **Rocket Juice Details**: The Lesson A rocket code implements a parachute pop sound, exhaust flame animations, and breadcrumb trails that weren't strictly outlined in initial requirements but were added to address device feedback.

### 3. Differences in HOW something works
* **Moon Target Feedback**: The derived outline asserts in its second part that the Moon has no target feedback. In our final **v1.4.2** implementation, we successfully added concentric dwell feedback and celebration reveal rings around the Moon coordinates, meaning the Moon is now visually on par with the planets.

## Part 2: Analysis of the 11 Audit Findings

We have verified each of the 11 findings against the

It seems I need to adjust `onDrag` to cancel the drag when `resetting` is true, to solve this race condition. I will keep this in mind for the discrepancy report. <USER_REQUEST>
did i send these?
Let me first fact-check the one claim in their report that contradicts my read — "Moon target feedback already fixed in v1.4.2." When I read the file an hour ago there were no Moon rings, so either they've edited again since, or they're confabulating:

Their report checks out — including the part where it corrects *me*. Here's the full intent audit:

## First, an honest correction: my finding #5 was wrong

Antigravity's "already fixed in v1.4.2" claim about Moon feedback is **correct, and my outline was wrong**. The Moon dwell + celebration rings exist at [SkyViewScreen.kt:647](app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt:647) — I missed them because my verification read stopped at line 615 (the Moon block starts at 640) and my confirmation grep required "moon" and "ring" on the *same line*, which never happens in that block. Two sloppy checks compounding into a confident false positive — exactly the failure mode these cross-audits exist to catch. Score one for the two-agent loop.

## Verdict on the discrepancy report: accurate, but it audited against the wrong baseline

All four of its "Part 1 discrepancies" are real differences from *its prompt history* — but **none of them are discrepancies against recorded intent**, because every one was a deliberate, dated decision already written into the dashboard:

| Antigravity's "discrepancy" | Actual status in the intent record |
|---|---|
| Mars flag/orbit-insertion animation missing | Dashboard task note: *"simplified: fanfare + confetti + sticker + the line; no flag/orbit-insertion animation in v1"* — conscious simplification |
| "Sky default" toggle dropped | Dashboard P5 note: *"sky default dropped — drag is always the default, magic window is a per-session toggle"* — decided |
| Dormant ConsequenceDetector states | Dashboard: *"unreachable by construction; detector + tests stay for future modules"* — decided at the Place-to-Orbit pivot |
| Unrequested rocket "juice" | v1.3.3 changelog entry — your device feedback, logged |

**The real finding of this whole exercise:** Antigravity is treating its prompt transcript as the source of truth, when the living source of truth is `docs/sophiaspace-prd.md` + the dashboard's decisions log and changelog, which have absorbed every pivot. Tell it that, and this class of false discrepancy disappears.

## Intent audit conclusion

Code vs. recorded intent: **aligned everywhere I can check.** The only two places code delivers less than the original PRD spec are the two consciously-simplified items above — both awaiting your taste, not fixes. Its Part 2 table is also accurate: findings 1–4, 6–11 confirmed (matching my own assessment and severity), #5 resolved, and its proposed fixes for each are the right ones — including the detail that #8's fix is resetting `_accuracy` in `stop()`, and that #2 needs the `size > 1` guard.

## What's actually left to do

Three real fixes: **TTS failure fallback** (critical — a mute device bricks the whole experience), **back-press guard at Home** (critical, one line), **drag-during-tween** (high, one guard in `onDrag`). Three worthwhile mediums: re-duck in-flight sounds, reset stale sensor accuracy, sticker-shelf overflow. "# Implementation Plan: Reconciled Code Audit Fixes for v1.4.3 (build 11)

This plan implements the six verified code/UX defects identified in the recent fresh-eyes audit.

## User Review Required

> [!NOTE]
> All changes are minor UX and stability improvements and adhere to the strict privacy and pedagogical constraints of the app. The next release will be versioned as **v1.4.3 (build 11)**.

## Open Questions

None.

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Robust TTS Initialization and Fallback

#### [MODIFY] [NarrationManager.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/audio/NarrationManager.kt)
- Keep track of an `initFailed` boolean flag.
- If TTS fails to initialize (`status != TextToSpeech.SUCCESS`) or if `Locale.US` is not supported, set `initFailed = true` and show a Toast warning: *"Text-to-Speech failed to start. Please check your system US-English voice settings!"*.
- When `speakText` is called, check if `initFailed` is true. If so, attempt to re-initialize TTS, providing an automatic retry mechanism if the TTS engine becomes available later.

---

### 2. Navigation Back-Press Crash Guard

#### [MODIFY] [Navigation.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/Navigation.kt)
- Guard the system back-press event for `Home` to only pop the backstack if it contains more than just the home screen:
  ```kotlin
  onBack = { if (backStack.size > 1) backStack.removeLastOrNull() }
  ```

---

### 3. Drag-During-Reset Sandbox Guard

#### [MODIFY] [SandboxScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/sandbox/SandboxScreen.kt)
- Guard the pointer input drag loop so that dragging is ignored and aborted when the reset tween is actively running:
  - In `onDrag`: return early if `resetting` is true.
  - In `release(b)` helper: if `resetting` is true, clean up

<truncated 453 bytes>
FOV read from CameraCharacteristics (focal length + sensor size) at bind time; (2) sensor smoothing tuned for the drawn sky added ~100ms of swim against the live image — AR now uses a snappier EMA (0.45 vs 0.18); (3) latent bug found en route: the dwell loop captured the star tables from screen-open and never saw the per-second refreshes — fixed with rememberUpdatedState. Constellation finding now triggers on ANY member star (the Dipper spans ~25°, far past the old 10°-from-anchor rule). Planets got name labels, dwell-find with spoken kid facts ("That\'s Jupiter — the BIGGEST planet of all!"), celebration rings, and a 🪐 sticker each. 56/56 tests (new exact-tangent projection test). Published SophiasSpace-1.4.1.apk + pushed to the Pixel over wireless adb.'},
   {date:'2026-06-11', title:'v1.4.0 (build 8) — "Real sky" camera AR mode', body:'Tom requested camera AR after device testing — a deliberate reversal of the v1 zero-permission non-goal, recorded in the decisions log. Third sky mode cycled by the existing chip: Drag → Magic window → 📷 Real sky. CameraX PreviewView sits under the unchanged star overlay (the OrientationProvider look vector is literally the back camera\'s optical axis, so alignment came free, declination correction included); permission asked once on first entry, denial quietly stays in Magic window; camera binds only while AR is active and unbinds on mode change/backgrounding (privacy dot off); AR skips the dark background + ground fill and widens the projection FOV to ~70° to match the camera. New SKY_AR_ON narration line. Manifest now carries exactly one permission (CAMERA, preview-only) — PRD, decisions, and the site card updated honestly. 55/55 tests green. Published SophiasSpace-1.4.0.apk (14.5 MB — CameraX adds ~2 MB).'},
[diff_block_end]

1: # Implementation Plan: Reconciled Code Audit Fixes for v1.4.3 (build 11)
2: 
3: This plan implements the six verified code/UX defects identified in the recent fresh-eyes audit.
4: 
5: ## User Review Required
6: 
7: > [!NOTE]
8: > All changes are minor UX and stability improvements and adhere to the strict privacy and pedagogical constraints of the app. The next release will be versioned as **v1.4.3 (build 11)**.
9: 
10: ## Open Questions
11: 
12: None.
13: 
14: ## Proposed Changes
15: 
16: ### 1. Robust TTS Initialization and Fallback
17: 
18: #### [MODIFY] [NarrationManager.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/audio/NarrationManager.kt)
19: - Keep track of an `initFailed` boolean flag.
20: - If TTS fails to initialize (`status != TextToSpeech.SUCCESS`) or if `Locale.US` is not supported, set `initFailed = true` and show a Toast warning: *"Text-to-Speech failed to start. Please check your system US-English voice settings!"*.
21: - When `speakText` is called, check if `initFailed` is true. If so, attempt to re-initialize TTS, providing an automatic retry mechanism if the TTS engine becomes available later.
22: 
23: ---
24: 
25: ### 2. Navigation Back-Press Crash Guard
26: 
27: #### [MODIFY] [Navigation.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/Navigation.kt)
28: - Guard the system back-press event for `Home` to only pop the backstack if it contains more than just the home sc

/HomeScreen.kt)

81: ## Verification Plan
82: 
83: ### Automated Tests
84: - Run `.\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest` to verify all 57 tests remain green.
85: 
86: ### Manual Verification

args.ArtifactMetadata: {"RequestFeedback":false,"Summary":"Diagnostic port scanner for wireless ADB.","UserFacing":false}
args.CodeContent:
"import asyncio
import sys

async def try_connect(ip, port):
    try:
        conn = asyncio.open_connection(ip, port)
        reader, writer = await asyncio.wait_for(conn, timeout=1.5)
        writer.close()
        await writer.wait_closed()
        print(f"FOUND:{port}", flush=True)
        return port
    except Exception:
        return None

async def main():
    ip = "192.168.50.67"
    start_port = 30000
    end_port = 50000
    batch_size = 200
    
    print(f"Scanning {ip} from {start_port} to {end_port}...", flush=True)
    
    for i in range(start_port, end_port, batch_size):
        if i % 2000 == 0:
            print(f"Progress: reached port {i}...", flush=True)
        batch_tasks = [try_connect(ip, port) for port in range(i, min(i + batch_size, end_port))]
        results = await asyncio.gather(*batch_tasks)
        found = [r for r in results if r is not None]
        if found:
            print(f"Scan complete. Active port: {found[0]}", flush=True)
            sys.exit(0)
            
    print("No open ports found.", flush=True)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())
"
args.Description: "Write diagnostic port scanner."

args.CodeContent:
"import asyncio
import sys

async def try_connect(ip, port):
    try:
        conn = asyncio.open_connection(ip, port)
        reader, writer = await asyncio.wait_for(conn, timeout=1.0)
        writer.close()
        await writer.wait_closed()
        print(f"FOUND:{ip}:{port}", flush=True)
        return ip, port
    except Exception:
        return None

async def scan_ip(ip):
    start_port = 30000
    end_port = 50000
    batch_size = 200
    print(f"Scanning {ip} from {start_port} to {end_port}...", flush=True)
    for i in range(start_port, end_port, batch_size):
        batch_tasks = [try_connect(ip, port) for port in range(i, min(i + batch_size, end_port))]
        results = await asyncio.gather(*batch_tasks)
        found = [r for r in results if r is not None]
        if found:
            print(f"Found active ADB port on {ip}: {found[0][1]}", flush=True)
            return found[0]
    return None

async def main():
    ips = ["192.168.50.72", "192.168.50.163", "192.168.50.193", "192.168.50.204"]
    for ip in ips:
        res = await scan_ip(ip)
        if res:
            print(f"SUCCESS: {res[0]}:{res[1]}")
            sys.exit(0)
    print("No ADB port found on any of the target IPs.")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())
"

args.CodeContent:
"# Walkthrough: Audit Findings and Release v1.4.3 (build 11)

This document details the audit fixes, verification results, and release deployment for version **v1.4.3 (build 11)** of Sophia's Space.

## 🛠️ Changes Implemented

### 1. Robust TTS Initialization and Fallback
- **Modified**: [NarrationManager.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/audio/NarrationManager.kt)
- **Fix**: Added tracking of `initFailed`. If the TTS engine fails to initialize (or if the US English locale is unavailable), a Toast message is displayed: *"Text-to-Speech failed to start. Please check your system US-English voice settings!"*. Additionally, if `speakText` is subsequently called and the engine is in a failed state, it attempts to re-initialize TTS automatically, recovery-guarding subsequent narration requests.

### 2. Navigation Back-Press Crash Guard
- **Modified**: [Navigation.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/Navigation.kt)
- **Fix**: Guarded the system back-press callback on the `Home` screen so that it only pops the backstack if it contains more than one screen (`backStack.size > 1`). This prevents popping the root home screen, which was causing Jetpack Navigation3 to crash when pressing back on the home dashboard.

### 3. Drag-During-Reset Sandbox Guard
- **Modified**: [SandboxScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/sandbox/SandboxScreen.kt)
- **Fix**: Added logic to ignore and abort planet drag gestures when the reset tween is active. Checked inside `onDrag` and the `release()` helper to prevent user touch input from overriding target coordinates during tween animation.

### 4. Dynamic SoundPool Re-Ducking
- **Modified**: [SoundSynth.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophi
<truncated 2787 bytes>

9: ## What the app is (as the code tells it)
10: 
11: **"Sophia's Space"** (`com.example.sophiaspace`) — a single-activity Jetpack Compose Android app (minSdk 24, targetSdk 36, portrait-locked, always-dark space theme, `allowBackup=false`). One permission: CAMERA, used solely as a live preview layer. Every interaction speaks through a single shared TextToSpeech engine with playful first-person-plural lines addressed to a child named Sophia ("Welcome to space, Sophia!") — the app is unmistakably built for **one specific pre-reader child**: text is decorative, meaning is carried by voice, touch targets are huge, and nothing has a fail state.
12:

io/SoundSynth.kt`)
50: 5. **The Moon dwell-find has no target feedback.** Planets get a growing gold ring during dwell and a celebration ring on find; the Moon target gets neither — only the center reticle fills. Inconsistent with how the rest of finding teaches itself. (`ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt`)
51: 6. **"Once per session" welcome is actually once per process.** `welcomedThisSession` is a top-level var: warm relaunches never re-welcome; a mid-session process death re-welcomes. (`ui/home/HomeScreen.kt`)
52: 7. **AR field-of-view math assumes Pixel-typical camera behavior** — default 4:3 full-active-array preview with FILL_CENTER cropping. Devices where CameraX selects a different stream aspect or a cropped sensor will get a mis-scaled overlay; there is no runtime cross-check. (`ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt`)
53: 8. **Stale sensor-accuracy can fire one spurious calibration prompt.** `OrientationProvider._accuracy` retains its last value across `stop()`; re-entering a sensor mode can re-trigger the lazy-8 line off old data before a fresh reading lands (debounce limits it to once). (`sky/OrientationProvider.kt`)
54: 9. **Sky catalog accuracy is untested by construction.** Tests verify referential integrity (every line endpoint exists), not that RA/Dec values match the real sky — coordinate errors would pass CI invisibly and only show up against the actual night sky.
55: 10. **Whole-screen recomposition at 60 fps is the rendering strategy.** Sandbox, lessons, and Sky View all write a `frameTick` state every frame and read it in composition; it works, but it's the app's largest performance liability and is load-bearing — "optimizing away" the reads breaks redrawing. Handle with care.
56: 11. **The sticker shelf has no overflow plan.** 27 earnable stickers (7 lesson + 12 constellations + 7 planets + moon) join one wrapping Text line that compresses the Home grid on small screens as the collection grows. (`ui/home/HomeScreen.kt`)

10: # Sophia's Space — A Solar System for a 5-Year-Old
11: 
12: ## Executive statement
13: 
14: Sophia's Space is a fully narrated, exploratory Android app that lets a 5-year-old pre-reader *feel* how the solar system works — not by reading about it, but by spinning the Earth with her finger, dragging the Moon out of its orbit and watching it fly away, launching rockets that miss Mars until she learns to aim where Mars is *going to be*, and holding the phone up to the real night sky to find the constellations hiding there. Everything speaks. Nothing punishes. Every broken thing can be put back.
15: 
16: The big ideas, at her level:
17: 
18: | Big idea | How she experiences it |
19: |---|---|
20: | Everything in space is spinning and circling | Planets loop the Sun on glowing trails; she can speed time up and watch a year happen |
21: | A day is the Earth spinning | She spins Earth with her finger and watches her little house go from sunshine to stars |
22: | Moon phases are sunlight + shadow | She drags the Moon around Earth and a "what we see from home" window shows the phase change live |
23: | Gravity holds it all together — and has consequences | A real (simplified) gravity simulation: move Earth closer to the Sun and it sweats; fling the Moon and it escapes |
24: | Rockets curve, and missions aim ahead | Straight up falls back down; sideways-fa
<truncated 7639 bytes>
 (designed-for, not built):** more constellations, seasonal-tilt lesson, parent-recorded voiceover, tablet layout, camera AR.

82: ## Risk register
83: 
84: | Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
85: |---|---|---|
86: | Rotation-vector sensor noisy/absent; magnetometer interference indoors | Medium | Phase 0 spike on real device; quaternion slerp smoothing; kid-friendly figure-8 calibration ("draw a lazy 8 in the air!"); drag mode is first-class, not a fallback |
87: | Azimuth needs declination + location | Certain | One-time city pick in grown-up settings; generous capture radii absorb ±10° |
88: | Physics blowup when she drops bodies onto each other | High — kids find it instantly | Softening, velocity clamp, world bounds, sub-step cap; JVM stress test (1000 random drags, assert no NaN/Inf) |
89: | TTS latency or robotic voice losing her | Medium | Warm-up at launch, buffered queue, short rhythmic lines; parent-recorded VO is the future option |
90: | Two sandbox scales (Sun vs Earth–Moon) confusing | Medium | Distinct backdrops, "let's zoom waaay in" framing narration; validate at GATE 1 |
91: | Drag-fling too imprecise for 5-yo motor skills | Medium | GATE 1 pivot: tap-body-then-arrow aiming (shares the rocket aim-arrow code) |
92: | Canvas star field performance | Low | ~450-star cap, batched drawPoints, dot-product culling, no per-frame allocation |
93: | Battery drain (sensor + 60 fps canvas) | Low | SENSOR_DELAY_GAME only on Sky View; unregister on pause; sim pauses when backgrounded |
94: | Scope creep across 6 modules | High | Gates are explicit stop points; Phases 3 and 4 independently shippable |

# Merge & Brainstorming Task: Sophia's Space (v1.4.3) + Preschool & Emotional Learning

I am working on a research and design project to merge a custom Android application I built for my 5-year-old daughter, Sophia (called "Sophia's Space"), with emotional/social-emotional learning (SEL) and preschool-readiness concepts. 

Below is the complete technical and pedagogical outline of "Sophia's Space" (as of version v1.4.3 / build 11), including its architecture, design rules, screen functionalities, and latest engine updates. 

Read this context carefully. Once you understand the app, I want you to act as a Senior Child Development Pedagogy Expert and Mobile Software Architect to help me brainstorm a merged application.

---

## 1. Core Pedagogical & Design Rules (The Code's Philosophy)
The codebase strictly enforces the following principles to ensure the app is a safe, encouraging, and educationally honest toy:
1. **Curiosity is Never Punished**: There are zero fail states, zero "game over" screens, and no resource limits (like fuel). Everything is completely reversible.
2. **"Put It Back!"**: If things get cluttered or fly away, a single prominent button smoothly animates all bodies back to their starting positions.
3. **Honest Science under Cartoon Visuals**: The app uses a real gravity simulator, real star catalogs (54 bright stars), real constellation lines, and real Keplerian ephemeris math for planet coordinates. The simplifications are honest (e.g. scale is adjusted, but relative orbits and orbital mechanics are true).
